claim: YOLO harness boundary audit implementation covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and excludes only the dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function implements dual-mode detection: git ls-files for git repos, recursive walkFiles fallback for non-git
evidence.why: This is the core boundary audit implementation that builds file digests for mutation detection.

evidence.summary: gitFiles uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' to get tracked, cached, and other files excluding standard patterns
evidence.why: This command captures the full set of files git tracks, including staged and untracked files.

evidence.summary: walkFiles provides recursive filesystem fallback with excludedDir filter for .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: This ensures non-git projects and git blindspots are covered while excluding build artifacts.

evidence.summary: Combines git and walked files with deduplication: [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot)])]
evidence.why: This union ensures both git-known files and filesystem walk results are covered.

evidence.summary: Exclusion logic skips allowedRoots (artifact directories): if (allowed.some((root) => isWithin(absolute, root))) continue
evidence.why: This implements the artifact root exclusion requirement from the demand.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after digests to detect added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: This is the mutation detection logic that identifies boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Runtime takes boundary snapshots before and after agent execution, passes output_dir as allowedRoot, and injects blockers on any detected mutations
evidence.why: This shows the complete boundary audit workflow in action.

evidence.summary: Test validates boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: This proves the boundary audit is enforced regardless of agent output success.

evidence.summary: Test validates git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations
evidence.why: This proves untracked file detection works in git repositories.

evidence.summary: Test validates git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: This proves gitignored files are detected unless in excluded directories.

evidence.summary: Test validates non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: This proves non-git fallback works and artifact exclusion is enforced.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states dispatch runs boundary audits before/after agent execution and blockers any target-project mutation while only allowing artifact root writes
evidence.why: This is the product requirement specification for boundary audit behavior.

evidence.summary: Stress test scenario 'git-untracked-boundary' validates the exact claim with objective matching the demand text
evidence.why: This shows the demand is being actively stress-tested against real Claude agents.

risks: If boundary snapshot logic has gaps, full-tool agents could mutate project files without triggering blockers
If git ls-files flags don't cover all file states, some mutations might go undetected
If excludedDirs list is incomplete, build artifacts could create false positives or performance issues

result.notes: Implementation matches acceptance criteria: boundary snapshots use git ls-files for tracked/cached/other files, walkFiles fallback for non-git, and exclude only the dispatch artifact root. Test coverage validates all mutation types across both git and non-git scenarios.
